title: [InstCombine] Relax one-use check for min/max(fpext x, fpext y) to fpext(min/max(x, y)) fold
author: nikic
description: If only of the operands is one-use, the total number of fpexts stays the same, but the min/max is performed on a narrowed type. Additionally, the fpext may fold with a following fptrunc.

tests.test_body:
; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare double @llvm.maxnum.f64(double, double) #0

define float @reduce_precision_multi_use_2(float %x, float %y, ptr %p, ptr %p2) {
  %x.ext = fpext float %x to double
  %y.ext = fpext float %y to double
  store double %x.ext, ptr %p, align 8
  store double %y.ext, ptr %p2, align 8
  %maxnum = call double @llvm.maxnum.f64(double %x.ext, double %y.ext)
  %trunc = fptrunc double %maxnum to float
  ret float %trunc
}

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none) }

}
